commit.message:
Fix #198: Detect and reject tied-slowest-time in Phase 1/2 elimination

When multiple players share the slowest course time, emit a
descriptive "Tie detected" error (a business logic error) so that
the admin can resolve it manually rather than silently eliminating
a player at random based on sort order.

- finals-phase-manager.ts: count ties before eliminating; throw on >1
- phases/route.ts: add "Tie detected" to recognized business errors
- finals-phase-manager.test.ts: add test for tie scenario

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Week 9 summary: Graphs!

Covers Voronoi analysis wrap-up, BFS/graph connection, and all 7 graph
application domains from Thursday (Internet, Conflict, Complete, Social
Network, Rush Hour, State Machine, Stanford Bunny). Includes Handshaking
Lemma, edge bounds, Havel-Hakimi, and Euler path theorem. Fill-in-the-blank
answers embedded throughout.

commit.message:
fix: force dark mode via Tailwind v4 custom variant

Tailwind v4 defaults to @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) for the
dark: variant, ignoring the .dark class on <html>. Users with OS light
mode saw white Card backgrounds with invisible light-gray text.

Add @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *)) so dark: variants
activate via the .dark class already present on <html>, ensuring
consistent dark theme regardless of OS preference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Product edit page freeze from double-encoded features JSON

Features field was stored as a double-encoded JSON string in the database,
causing Alpine.js to iterate 562 individual characters instead of the
actual feature array. This created 562 input fields and froze the page.

- Fix edit() to decode double-encoded features before passing to view
- Add JS safety check to parse string features on client side
- Filter empty feature strings on store/update
- Reorganize edit form into tabbed UI for better performance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rank team leaderboard by executed volume instead of activity count

- team.service.js: track executedCount and executedVolume per user, sort by volume
- Team.jsx: display executed volume as primary metric with trade count and total activities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mobile card view: use position:fixed to bypass height chain

height:100% through map-container (overflow:visible) → map-layout
was resolving to 0 in some browsers, making cards invisible.
position:fixed takes the card overlay out of the document flow
entirely — no parent height chain, no nested overflow conflicts,
touch events go directly to the fixed element without competing
with the page's vertical scroll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

README tagline. Fix sort picker help text in TUI.

commit.message:
fix: address code review — namerefs, Makefile packaging, output consistency

Critical fixes:
- Replace all eval calls in trimv with declare -n namerefs (security)
- Replace trimv temp file stdin accumulation with string concatenation
- Add DESTDIR support and derive SHAREDIR/COMPDIR from PREFIX
- Replace GNU Make $(foreach) with portable shell loops
- Fix assertion count and test suite count in README

Output consistency:
- Replace echo -n with printf '%s\n' across all utilities
- Arg mode now outputs trailing newline, matching stdin mode
- Remove cargo-cult three-space replacement in squeeze
- Document word-split normalization trick in trimall

Housekeeping:
- Fix ltrim heredoc delimiter (EOT → HELP) for consistency
- Clean up .gitignore (remove redundant/noisy entries)
- Update share paths from /usr/share to /usr/local/share
- Add manpage and bash completion to install targets

New test suites:
- test-locale: LC_ALL=C vs en_US.UTF-8, NBSP preservation
- test-sigpipe: SIGPIPE handling with head -1, hang detection
- test-concurrent: parallel trimv, rapid sequential, large stdin
- test-null-byte: null byte handling, binary-like input

All 20 test suites pass (190+ assertions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Enhancement: Lower Phase 2 combination threshold from 2 to 1 (#1734) (#1740)

* Enhancement: Lower Phase 2 combination threshold from 2 to 1 (#1734)

When Phase 1 produces only 1 successful single candidate, supplement with
historically successful candidates from the success cache to enable Phase 2
combination building. Previously, Phase 2 was skipped entirely unless 2+
Phase 1 singles succeeded.

- Add SupplementFromCache module to load and validate cache entries
- Lower Phase 2 gate from >= 2 to >= 1 successful singles
- Validate cached entries against current creature (neuron/synapse existence)
- Filter out already-applied, duplicate, and combo entries
- Maintain existing behaviour when 2+ Phase 1 successes exist

commit.message:
Editorial redesign + fix mobile drag-and-drop reordering

Visual overhaul:
- Playfair Display serif font for masthead, brief title, and headlines
- Warm cream text (#ede9df) on near-black bg — newsprint feel
- Thin 3px gold accent stripe at top of viewport
- Category labels: small-caps text with per-category color, no pill
- Thin colored left border on each card for quick visual scanning
- Headlines in Playfair Display serif (editorial, not generic UI)
- Bullets styled with em-dash instead of arrow
- Sources rendered as italic byline (Via Reuters · FT) not pills
- Story dividers: centered '· · ·' newspaper-style
- Settings panel: Playfair heading, muted gold toggle style
- Border-radius 4px throughout (more formal, less rounded-generic)
- Settings panel slides up from bottom on mobile (full-width)

Mobile drag fix:
- Replace HTML5 drag events with Pointer Events API (pointerdown/
  pointermove/pointerup) — works on both touch and mouse
- setPointerCapture on drag handle keeps events firing while
  finger moves across other rows
- touch-action: none on handle prevents scroll interference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Introduce project management with dedicated working folder, persistent

Update README with projects documentation, new screenshot, and changelog.

commit.message:
fix: Intel Signals and Sentiment panels black in light mode

Root cause: applyTheme() only toggled dark-mode class, never added
light-mode class to body — so all body.light-mode CSS rules were dead.

- app.js: applyTheme now also toggles light-mode class on body
- index.html: add missing light-mode overrides for correlate-title,
  signal-count, signal-countries, sentiment-region, sentiment-updated
  so all Tier-3 sidebar panel elements render correctly in both themes
